title: Remote Sensing Technicians
description: Apply remote sensing technologies to assist scientists in areas such as natural resources, urban planning, or homeland security. May prepare flight plans or sensor configurations for flight trips.

tasks.text: Collect geospatial data, using technologies such as aerial photography, light and radio wave detection systems, digital satellites, or thermal energy systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify integrity and accuracy of data contained in remote sensing image analysis systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Integrate remotely sensed data with other geospatial data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Consult with remote sensing scientists, surveyors, cartographers, or engineers to determine project needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Adjust remotely sensed images for optimum presentation by using software to select image displays, define image set categories, or choose processing routines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manipulate raw data to enhance interpretation, either on the ground or during remote sensing flights.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Merge scanned images or build photo mosaics of large areas, using image processing software.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in the planning or development of mapping projects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare documentation or presentations, including charts, photos, or graphs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Correct raw data for errors due to factors such as skew or atmospheric variation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calibrate data collection equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop or maintain geospatial information databases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor raw data quality during collection, and make equipment corrections as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain records of survey data.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Evaluate remote sensing project requirements to determine the types of equipment or computer software necessary to meet project requirements, such as specific image types or output resolutions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Collect verification data on the ground, using equipment such as global positioning receivers, digital cameras, or notebook computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Document methods used and write technical reports containing information collected.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop specialized computer software routines to customize and integrate image analysis.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with agricultural workers to apply remote sensing information to efforts to reduce negative environmental impacts of farming practices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect remote sensing data for forest or carbon tracking activities involved in assessing the impact of environmental change.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Provide remote sensing data for use in addressing environmental issues, such as surface water modeling or dust cloud detection.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
